title: Arrest and extradition of Scattered Spider hacker shines light on how Windows telemetry GDIDs can identify and track users Microsoft device identifier is just one digital fingerprint in a software world rife with them
summary: The arrest of a Scattered Spider hacker highlights Windows telemetry's role in tracking users. Microsoft's device identifier, GDID, is just one example of the various digital fingerprints used to identify individuals online. This has significant implications for user privacy and online security.
insight: GDID is not the only digital fingerprint in a software world rife with tracking.

title: Space Force gets first mobile high-powered electromagnetic beam weapon to cripple enemy satellites plans to deploy 32 Meadowlands units to detect, deny, disrupt, and degrade hostile space assets
summary: The US Space Force has received the first Meadowlands electronic warfare system, designed to disrupt hostile satellites.
insight: Enhanced space warfare capabilities to counter enemy satellite threats.

title: Unannounced Nvidia RTX 50 Super GPUs appear in Seasonic PSU calculator unreleased graphics cards shown with 10-17% higher TGP over original models
summary: Unannounced Nvidia RTX 50 Super GPUs appear in Seasonic PSU calculator, potentially featuring 10-17% higher TGP over original models, providing insight into unreleased graphics cards.
insight: Potential power increase may impact system build and cooling requirements.

title: South Korea's $880 billion chip and AI plan faces big power and water challenges a single megacluster requires a quarter of Seoul's total power demand
summary: South Korea plans a massive $880 billion chip and AI initiative, which includes a $520 billion semiconductor program, but faces significant power and water challenges due to the massive energy demands of a single megacluster, equivalent to a quarter of Seoul's total power demand.
insight: Massive energy demands threaten South Korea's ambitious chip and AI plan.

title: Chinese memory and storage firm expected to post more than 60,000% jump in profits due to exploding demand Lexar owner Longsys forecasts nearly $1.5 billion profit for 1H26 compared to $2.1 million last year
summary: Chinese memory and storage firm Longsys forecasts a massive profit jump due to the AI-driven chip shortage, expecting nearly $1.5 billion in profit for the first half of 2026 compared to $2.1 million last year.
insight: Chip shortage drives massive profit surge in memory and storage industry.

title: AVX-512 support is reportedly returning with Intel's next-gen Nova Lake CPUs Latest Linux kernel patches reveal P-cores and E-cores will gain native 512-bit execution
summary: Intel's Nova Lake CPUs will reportedly feature native 512-bit execution, including E-cores. This means AVX-512 support is returning to Intel's client CPUs. The upcoming desktop lineup will benefit from improved performance.
insight: Enhanced performance for compute-intensive tasks and applications.

title: Companies are now using automatic Windows installers to display Adware through the Microsoft Store when you install new hardware customer immediately gets McAfee ads on their PC after connecting new LG monitor; here's how to block the new ads
summary: LG monitors auto-install an app on PCs via Microsoft Store, displaying ads. This includes McAfee ads when new hardware is connected, posing a concern for user privacy and data security. Users can block the ads by following specific steps.
insight: Unauthorized app installation compromises user control over their PCs.

title: PlayStation disc petition approaches 200,000 signatures as backlash grows over Sony's decision to stop producing new physical media firm still plans to produce optical media for existing titles, but new games will be digital only
summary: A Change.org petition against Sony's decision to stop physical media has gathered over 172,000 signatures, while the company still plans to produce optical media for existing titles but will make new games digital only.
insight: Gaming community pushback highlights shift towards digital gaming consumption.

title: Steam Machines with the Red Line of Death get a simple, official cure: Clear the CMOS clearing the CMOS can revive flat(red)-lining cubes
summary: Valve provides simple CMOS clearing instructions to revive affected Steam Machines with RLOD. The process involves a straightforward reset of the motherboard's CMOS settings. This solution aims to resolve the notorious Red Line of Death issue.
insight: CMOS clearing offers a quick fix for RLOD Steam Machine problems

title: UK gives data centers option to apply for 'national importance' status that overrides local regulations, cuts timeline by a year eligible projects to bypass local councils, save more than a billion dollars in NIMBY fights
summary: The UK government allows data centers to bypass local council approvals, speeding up developments by up to a year and saving over a billion dollars. The move targets nationally significant infrastructure projects to streamline the process. This is expected to benefit large-scale projects like data centers.
insight: Streamlined regulations boost data center development and cost efficiency.

title: Hands-on with Corsair's 2800X RS-R ARGB Micro-ATX PC Case smaller footprint, roomy internals, includes three fans
summary: The Corsair 2800X RS-R ARGB is a compact MicroATX case with room for full-size hardware, strong cooling support, and three pre-installed ARGB fans, offering solid value for a small-form-factor chassis at an affordable price under $90.
insight: Compact design meets full-size hardware needs at budget-friendly prices.

title: Dev ports Linux to Atari's notorious Jaguar console from 1993 the first 64-bit console features 2MB of RAM, 13.3 MHz CPU, and Tom and Jerry co-processors; the Jag was notoriously difficult to program and flopped
summary: A developer has successfully ported Linux to the Atari Jaguar console, despite its notorious programming difficulties and limited hardware specifications.
insight: Linux port demonstrates the resilience of open-source software against hardware challenges

title: Rikomagic DS04 An Android 14 4K digital signage player powered by Rockchip RK3576 SoC
summary: Rikomagic DS04 is a 4K digital signage player running Android 14 on an octa-core Rockchip RK3576 processor, offering features like dual 4K HDMI ports, Gigabit Ethernet, and 4G LTE connectivity.
insight: Cost-effective 4K digital signage option for various applications and industries.

title: KKSB 10-inch and 19-inch rack panels target Raspberry Pi clusters, support Pi HATs and active coolers
summary: KKSB Cases introduces rack panels for Raspberry Pi clusters in standard enclosures, supporting various Pi boards, HATs, and active coolers for easier cable management and expanded functionality.
insight: Raspberry Pi clusters gain organized and scalable infrastructure support.

title: Bryan Johnson, the millionaire biohacker who wants to live forever, diagnosed with incurable autoimmune disease
summary: Bryan Johnson, a millionaire biohacker, was diagnosed with autoimmune gastritis. This condition affects 2-5% of people and can be hard to detect in its early stages. Johnson shared his diagnosis on Instagram.
insight: Millionaire biohacker's autoimmune diagnosis challenges his pursuit of eternal life.

title: An indie developer says Steam's refund policy cost his well-reviewed game 55,000 refunds
summary: Indie game Paddle Paddle Paddle earned 90% positive reviews on Steam but 21% refund rate, with over 55,000 refunds, due to Valve's lenient refund policy.
insight: Steam's refund policy incentivizes refund abuse, hurting small game developers financially.

title: LG and Alienware monitors caught auto-installing Windows adware
summary: LG and Alienware monitors have been caught auto-installing Windows adware, causing pop-up ads.
insight: Unintended software installation can compromise user data and security.

title: This German drone just hit 699 km/h, and its maker wants the world record to prove it
summary: Quantum Systems' Apex Recordhunter drone reached 699 km/h in internal testing, seeking official recognition.
insight: Breaks speed records, pushing drone technology to new limits.

title: Keychron is stepping outside keyboards with a $349 Thunderbolt 5 dock aimed at power users
summary: Keychron's new Thunderbolt 5 dock supports dual 8K displays or four 4K monitors, catering to power users.
insight: Thunderbolt 5's high bandwidth enables seamless multi-monitor setups with laptops.

title: The Steam Machine now has official Windows drivers
summary: Microsoft has officially released Windows drivers for the Steam Machine, covering GPU, Bluetooth, and wireless connectivity, allowing for seamless integration with Windows.
insight: Steam Machine now fully compatible with Windows operating system.

title: Intel's troubled 18A chip process is finally running smoothly
summary: Intel has resolved yield issues with its 18A chip process, enabling high-volume production. The company has achieved a defect density range of 0.1 to 0.2, typical for mature nodes. This progress makes production sustainable and economically viable.
insight: Intel's manufacturing efficiency boosts economic viability of 18A chip production

title: Australia's teen social media ban stumbles as platforms skip age checks
summary: Australia's social media ban for teens under 16 is being disregarded, as major platforms fail to implement age checks.
insight: Platforms' lax approach undermines efforts to protect young Australians online.

title: The FBI and Google just took down a botnet that hijacked 2 million smart TVs
summary: The FBI and Google took down the Popa botnet, which hijacked 2 million smart TVs. The malicious software development kit targeted low-cost Android-based devices, including smart TVs and streaming boxes. This allowed attackers to use the devices as proxy exit points.
insight: Malicious software kits can compromise entire networks through inexpensive devices.

title: Microsoft confirms Windows 11 bug can steal over 500GB of storage here's how to check and fix it
summary: A Windows 11 bug can steal over 500GB of storage due to an issue with the Capability Access Manager. This system component is tied to Microsoft's app permission controls and manages access to privacy-sensitive features. Users can check and fix the issue to prevent data loss.
insight: Storage theft occurs due to excessive Capability Access Manager data accumulation.

title: A free app lets Sony headphones do head tracking for racing and flight sims
summary: A free app enables head tracking for Sony headphones in gaming, eliminating the need for extra IR tracking hardware or VR headsets.
insight: Enhanced gaming experience without costly accessories

title: The open-source, DRM-free Open Printer shows off a working prototype
summary: The Open Printer project showcases a working prototype, challenging the status quo of the printing industry. This open-source, DRM-free device aims to make printing more accessible and affordable. The project's success could disrupt the market dominated by proprietary systems.
insight: Challenging the printing industry's DRM-locked ink cartridge business model.

title: Fake tech support, bogus crypto, and a gold pickup: how scammers stole $673,000 in three days
summary: Scammers stole $673,000 in three days using fake tech support and crypto schemes.
insight: Scammers exploit trust and ignorance to steal large sums quickly online.
